messages.content: Paraphrase (Also, we are on the process to submit some proposals, budget would be increase if we selected. )
Additionally, we are in the process of submitting proposals and the budget would be elevated if we were chosen.
Paraphrase (For 2024, when will the budget get approval? When can we start implement activities?)
When will the budget receive approval for 2024? When can we commence implementing activities?
Paraphrase (we started discussion on it since October 2023 hoping to have final update on budget in December 2023, so we can have budget approval on time for project implement activities)

We initiated discussions on the budget in October 2023 with the aim of obtaining the final update in December 2023. This timeline will allow us to secure budget approval in a timely manner for the implementation of project activities.
Paraphrase (Proposal timeframe is vary from each donor (can be 1-2 years), for budget planning we review annually)
The timeframe for proposals varies for each donor, typically ranging from 1 to 2 years. However, for our budget planning purposes, we conduct an annual review.

Paraphrase (ERIKS budget which remains in previous year cannot carry forward into new years unless we have concrete and clear justification provided to donor. SMP promise to ensure this budget is spent as agree planed.)

The budget allocated to ERIKS, if unutilized in the previous year, cannot be carried forward into subsequent years unless we provide a concrete and clear justification to the donor. SMP is committed to ensuring that this budget is spent according to the agreed plan.

Paraphrase (-	Some staff refuse to implement activities because those activities are not their core work. 
-	Guidelines seem to be strict and complicated with make or delay the process of implementing activities.
-	No focal person to work on some specific activities, for example Economic Empowerment.
-	Workload or ad hoc tasks for PM and staffs)

- Certain staff members are unwilling to carry out activities that are not directly related to their primary responsibilities.
- The guidelines appear to be stringent and intricate, which can impede or delay the implementation process of activities.
- There is a lack of a dedicated focal person to handle specific activities, such as Economic Empowerment.
- The workload or ad hoc tasks assigned to project managers and staff members.

Paraphrase (The CD updated about survivor alumni event. She presented about the purpose of conducting survivor alumni event and the result of the event. From her speech, the event happen beyond expectation as we were planning to have only several relevant staff to join. Surprisingly, all most every staff in PP joint this event. It is good opportunity and good time to conduct this activities this year. )

The CD provided an update on the survivor alumni event, explaining its purpose and sharing the outcomes. According to her presentation, the event surpassed our expectations as we initially intended to invite only a few relevant staff members. Surprisingly, almost every staff member in PP participated in the event. This presents a valuable opportunity and favorable timing to carry out this activity this year.

Paraphrase (She also informed about upcoming survivor alumni and survivor leadership event that will happen in December 2023. In that event, we plan to have around 100 participants including former survivors, former staffs and our partner such as ERIK, IJM and Save the Children etc.)

She also provided information about an upcoming survivor alumni and survivor leadership event scheduled for December 2023. The plan is to have approximately 100 participants, including former survivors, former staff members, and partner organizations such as ERIK, IJM, and Save the Children, among others.

Paraphrase (She also informed about upcoming survivor alumni and survivor leadership event that will happen in December 2023. In that event, we plan to have around 100 participants including former survivors, former staffs and our partner such as ERIK, IJM and Save the Children etc...)

She also gave an update on the forthcoming survivor alumni and survivor leadership event scheduled for December 2023. During this event, our objective is to gather approximately 100 attendees, consisting of former survivors, previous staff members, as well as our partner organizations like ERIK, IJM, and Save the Children, among others.
Paraphrase (CD introduced to group discussion on Stop, Start and Continue. Participants are divided into 4 groups. )
The CD introduced a group discussion on the "Stop, Start, and Continue" framework, dividing the participants into four groups.
Paraphrase (SMT and MT will review all the input from discussion, and consolidated. The consolidated point will be shared to all staff in the December meeting.)

The Senior Management Team (SMT) and Management Team (MT) will review and consolidate all the input gathered from the discussion. The resulting consolidated points will be shared with all staff members during the December meeting.
Paraphrase (NSSF will present the benefit of national insurances to all staff. PP staff are required to join face to face while NW team will join online)
NSSF will provide a presentation on the benefits of national insurances to all staff members. PPC staff members are required to attend in person, while the NW team will join the presentation online.

Paraphrase (Board decided select Peter to be interim of CEO until June 2024. From next week he will be starting his role as an interim CEO. However, he still keeps supporting program office. All country director will still reporting to him.)

The board has made the decision to appoint Peter as the interim CEO until June 2024. He will assume his new role as interim CEO starting next week, while continuing to provide support to the program office. All country directors will still report to him during this period.
Paraphrase (From the discussion in October, HI will discuss and bring some action to the program office implementation to ensure program are implement more effectively.)

Based on the discussions held in October, HI will engage in further deliberations and take actionable steps to enhance the implementation of programs within the program office. The aim is to ensure more effective program implementation.
Paraphrase (Brother Sophany suggests Peter to share the good practice, lesson learnt, outcome harvesting from other country to HC during his next visit.)
Brother Sophany proposes that Peter shares best practices, lessons learned, and harvested outcomes from other countries with the Humanitarian Coordinator (HC) during his upcoming visit.
Paraphrase (He suggest each program office respectively to share good practices to each other across country which is seem to be more effective.)
He suggests that each program office shares their successful practices with one another across countries, as this approach appears to be more effective.
Paraphrase (Last, the country director gave closing remark, appreciating everyone participation and she wish everyone )
Lastly, the country director delivered closing remarks, expressing appreciation for everyone's participation and extending well wishes to all.
Paraphrase (Last, the Country Director wrapped up for today’s meeting, gave closing remark, appreciating everyone participation and she wish everyone)
At the end of today's meeting, the Country Director concluded by delivering closing remarks, expressing gratitude for the participation of everyone and extending her well wishes to all.
